title: SecretStore backends: add inventory::submit! registration
description:
Add inventory::submit! calls in each of the 12 backends that implement SecretStore, registering them as BackendRegistration instances for open-world dispatch.

What: Enable from_uri to discover all SecretStore backends at runtime via inventory iteration.

Backends covered (12):
- secretx-env: read-only SecretStore
- secretx-file: writable SecretStore (register for BackendRegistration only; WritableBackendRegistration is separate bead)
- secretx-aws-sm: writable
- secretx-aws-ssm: writable
- secretx-azure-kv: writable
- secretx-bitwarden: read-only
- secretx-doppler: writable
- secretx-gcp-sm: writable
- secretx-hashicorp-vault: writable
- secretx-keyring: writable
- secretx-pkcs11: writable + signing (register here for SecretStore; signing registration is separate)
- secretx-wolfhsm: signing-capable but ALSO implements SecretStore (check code)

DO NOT register: secretx-aws-kms (signing-only, no SecretStore impl), secretx-local-signing (signing-only)

Algorithm for each backend crate's src/lib.rs:
1. Add use secretx_core::BackendRegistration; at top
2. Add use std::sync::Arc; if not already present
3. At module level (outside any fn), add:
   inventory::submit!(BackendRegistration {
       name: <BACKEND_NAME_STR>,
       factory: |uri: &str| {
           <BackendStruct>::from_uri(uri).map(|b| Arc::new(b) as Arc<dyn secretx_core::SecretStore>)
       },
   });
4. Backend name strings: env, file, aws-sm, aws-ssm, azure-kv, bitwarden, doppler, gcp-sm, vault, keyring, pkcs11, wolfhsm (check code first for wolfhsm)

Defensive programming rules:
- Factory closure must use the same URI string, not reconstruct it
- Cast must be explicit: Arc::new(b) as Arc<dyn SecretStore>
- No unwrap() in factory — from_uri returns Result, map preserves it
- Never log URI content in factory
- Verify each backend name string matches exactly what SecretUri::parse returns for that backend

Agent team: standard (Researcher + Implementer + Tester + Validator)
